title: lagenophora pumila
intro: Lagenophora pumila, also known as the New Zealand button daisy or dwarf Lagena, is a small, annual or perennial plant native to New Zealand and Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, due to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. Lagenophora pumila can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat skin conditions, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
